reason: Finding 1 (Oracle context omission) has medium impact: users on non-Oracle databases may blindly copy `duplicateErrorErrCode="1"` without understanding it is Oracle-specific, leading to misconfigured unique-constraint exception handling in production. Finding 2 (missing hints) has low impact only. Because at least one finding is medium, needs_improvement is true.

findings_assessment.location: s2 — 設定ファイル例 XML, BasicSqlStatementExceptionFactory コメント
findings_assessment.justification: The XML example shows `duplicateErrorErrCode="1"` without indicating it is the Oracle error code for unique constraint violations. A developer on PostgreSQL, MySQL, or another database who copies this configuration will silently use the wrong error code, causing `DuplicateStatementException` to never be thrown on actual unique-constraint violations — a subtle but serious production bug. The property description table clarifies the semantics of the property but does not warn that the example value is vendor-specific. Why it persisted: the Phase E output is byte-for-byte identical to the Phase D input, indicating the fix agent made no edits to the file at all.

Both `SqlPStatement` and `AppDbConnection` appear as class names in s2 prose (via `SqlPStatement#setFetchSize`, `SqlPStatement#setQueryTimeout`, `AppDbConnection#prepareStatement`). Their absence from s2 hints slightly reduces retrieval precision for queries that mention these class names — such queries might route to s1 instead. However, s2 already contains many other high-signal hints (`fetchSize`, `queryTimeout`, `BasicStatementFactory`, etc.) that would catch most relevant queries, so the practical retrieval degradation is minimal. Why it persisted: same reason as finding 1 — Phase E produced no changes to the file.
